items.title: Labor wins blue-ribbon seat of Morphett from Liberals

children.content: Liberal frontbencher Stephen Patterson loses his bid to be re-elected in the beachside seat of Morphett in the South Australian state election.

articles.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects

children.content: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

image.altText: A man with a blue shirt standing in a lane with other people behind

articles.title: Why isn't One Nation winning many seats in the SA election despite its high vote?

children.content: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

image.altText: A person voting on a sample House of Representatives ballot paper, with names like Apple and Peach

gallery.altText: A bald man with glasses wearing a suit jacket and a purple shirt

thumbnail.altText: A bald man with glasses wearing a suit jacket and a purple shirt

items.title: Demons overwhelm Blues in late MCG surge

children.content: The Demons complete an amazing comeback and pile pressure on Carlton coach Michael Voss, while West Coast celebrates an inspirational victory on the road against Port Adelaide.

image.altText: An excited Melbourne player roars in celebration after kicking a goal, as he is grabbed by a teammate.

items.title: Late wickets give South Australia edge in absorbing Shield final

children.content: South Australia strikes twice late on day four to snatch the momentum from Victoria in the Sheffield Shield final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: Three South Australian players celebrate a Victorian wicket in the Sheffield Shield final.

items.title: Mass casualty exercise in Adelaide draws details from recent events

children.content: South Australian emergency services conduct their largest mass casuality exercise in history in a bid to test coordination between agencies.

gallery.altText: A unit with smoke coming from it on a footpath with people lying nearby.
A robot on caterpillar wheels extends it arms towards a backpack on a footpath
Emergency service workers help people acting as patients.

items.title: Kings one win away from NBL title after beating 36ers

children.content: The Sydney Kings edge a step closer to winning a sixth NBL championship following a 106-93 defeat of the Adelaide 36ers in front of a record crowd.

image.altText: Kendric Davis shoots during the NBL championship series between the Sydney Kings and Adelaide 36ers.

items.title: City couple quit careers and take over outback pub

children.content: Burnt out from city life and careers in Brisbane, Liz Murphy and Norm Matau knew they couldn't keep going. So they left it all behind and started a new life as outback publicans.

image.altText: A man and woman stand behind a bar in a pub.

items.title: Dying woman opens up on facing mortality at age 26

children.content: Before ending her life through voluntary assisted dying, South Australian woman Annaliese Holland shares her final wishes and advice on living a full life.

gallery.altText: A young woman with long blonde hair wears a puffer jacket while sitting on a park bench, looking at the camera with a smile.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman with long blonde hair wears a puffer jacket while sitting on a park bench, looking at the camera with a smile.

items.title: Bush artist 'Pro' Hart remembered 20 years after death

children.content: On March 28, 2006, Broken Hill lost one of its most renowned residents in internationally acclaimed artist Kevin "Pro" Hart. He was, and still is, considered a widely popular artist, but his recognition was divisive to different audiences.

image.altText: A man in a paint splattered blue shirt and glasses holding a paint brush and a paint board next to a painting of masked figures

items.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues

children.content: The SA government and AFL are in discussions to try to keep fans flocking to Adelaide for next month's AFL Gather Round, as the global fuel crisis threatens to disrupt travel plans.

image.altText: Multiple yellow signs reading 'pump not in use' attached to petrol pumps at a station

items.title: A century ago, a Liberal Party disintegrated, and there are lessons for its namesake

children.content: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

image.altText: A painting depicting a crowd in a street

items.title: Hatchet attack on dog at SA golf course prompts public appeal

children.content: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information.
Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: A dog with stitches in its head.

items.title: 'There is still hope': Former detective reflects six months on from Gus's disappearance

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

gallery.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

thumbnail.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: Titans and Dragons fight for first NRL win of 2026

children.content: Neither St George Illawarra nor Gold Coast have won an NRL game in 2026 but with the two sides facing off to finish Round 4 somebody will break their duck. Follow live.

image.altText: A man is tackled during a rugby league match

items.title: Piastri finishes second, ill-timed safety car denies him victory in Japan

children.content: An ill-timed safety car has denied Oscar Piastri a stunning Japanese Grand Prix victory, with Italian Kimi Antonelli benefiting to secure successive wins.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri driving his McLaren Formula 1 car on track during a qualifying session

items.title: No transport relief for Sydneysiders as fuel prices surge

children.content: The NSW Liberals and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union (RTBU) have renewed calls for free public transport in response to surging fuel prices.

articles.title: Free public transport in Tasmania for three months to counter fuel crisis

children.content: The Tasmanian government says public transport will be free statewide for three months, starting on Monday, in response to surging fuel prices.

image.altText: Metro bus

articles.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April as fuel prices climb

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

items.title: Liberals dump Moira Deeming from ticket for next state election

children.content: Controversial Victorian MP Moira Deeming has lost her preselection battle for the state election, with Liberal Party members replacing her with business leader Dinesh Gourisetty.

image.altText: Moira Deeming appears serious as she walks through the parliament carpark, wearing a purple blazer.

items.title: 'It's a nightmare': Complaints pile up over Booking.com grievances

children.content: Hundreds of complaints against Booking.com are piling up as customers and hosts allege they have lost money, their property has been trashed and no-one is taking responsibility.

items.title: Confronting images of 'unprecedented' outback flood after cyclone

children.content: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

items.title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump draw crowds across US and Europe

children.content: Huge crowds rally against US President Donald Trump from coast to coast in what organisers hope will be the largest single-day protest in US history.

image.altText: A woman dressed as the Statue of Liberty taking part in the protest.

items.title: Charlie was at the airport to pick me up. We've been 'us' ever since

children.content: After cancelling a full Sri Lankan wedding and a string of "disastrous" relationships, Sashi Perera found herself opposite a man who made believing in him simple.

items.title: Why the 'perfect pair' of jeans remains an elusive concept

children.content: The enduring challenge of finding the "perfect pair" of jeans highlights the garment's cultural significance, and the opportunity to move toward more sustainable and more thoughtfully designed denim.

image.altText: A rack of denim jeans of various shades of blue on coathangers

items.title: Consider yourself a trivia nut? Have a crack at this quiz

children.content: We know you've come here looking for trivia glory but you're unlikely to find any today.

items.title: The extraordinary story of Australia's oldest living Olympian and his remarkable debut

children.content: Gordon "Wingnut" Ingate was twice denied time off work to compete in the Olympic Games in his 20s, but continued to excel as a sailor, eventually getting the chance to compete in his 40s at Munich 1972.

items.title: 'A lover of life': Mal Leyland eyes off one final trip before dementia decline

children.content: Adventurer and filmmaker Mal Leyland, of Ask the Leyland Brothers fame, shares his private health battle. But the TV legend is determined to retrace the cross-country trip that made him famous 60 years ago.

image.altText: A man in his early 80s wearing a collared shirt has his arm around his adult daughter, she wears black singlet and short hair

items.title: The unexpected type of man more women want to date

children.content: For the first time, more men than women are "marrying up" the education ladder, toppling a millennia-old tradition, an exclusive ABC analysis has revealed.

items.title: Vital Aboriginal records saved in the 70s by an astute WA archivist

children.content: Margaret Medcalf, who turns 100 this week, insisted that decades of Aboriginal welfare records be preserved in the state archives and not destroyed. Those documents have proved essential to connecting Aboriginal people with their family history.

image.altText: black and white photo of Margaret Medcalf in the archives in 1969

items.title: Australia has to attack misinformation about climate change, Senate report says

children.content: Australia needs to increase its efforts to fight back against misinformation about climate change, a Senate report says.

items.title: Family's living experiment reveals Australia's abysmal apartment design

children.content: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family decide to rent an apartment as a trial. Will high-rise living be a realistic alternative to a house as a place to bring up our child?

children.content: As an artificial intelligence revolution approaches, the government is responding to a huge opportunity in data-centre development while balancing copyright concerns and workforce anxiety.

items.title: Victorian government ordered to pay landowner $27m over rezoning

children.content: A court orders the Victorian government to pay more than $27 million in compensation to a landowner whose property was partially rezoned for public acquisition, after initially making the man a formal offer of $0.

image.altText: Exterior of a court.

items.title: Former AFL footballer asked how much he drank before he was allegedly raped

children.content: Daniel Hayes was 17 years old when he was allegedly raped by his coach while playing in an Australian Rules football talent league in Victoria.

items.title: Myanmar remains the world's invisible war

children.content: Five years after the Myanmar military coup, the country is still in a brutal civil war.
There have been reports of genocide, the burning of entire villages to the ground, and the indiscriminate killing of civilians.
So, what's happening in Myanmar now, and why has the conflict received so little global attention?
Reporting by Nicholas Maher for ABC News Loop.
